claim: Observability becomes a bottleneck at scale: success with agents drives adoption which generates an avalanche of feedback signals that human teams cannot process
actionable: Build observability and automated feedback processing into agent systems from the start—use AI-driven triage and clustering to handle signal volume at scale.
source_title: Observing And Testing CX Agents | Interrupt 26

claim: Design feedback loops as continuous with AI triage and code agents, not as ticket queues—use agents to automate diagnostics, clustering, and PR generation with human approval only on writes
actionable: Implement triage agents using MCPs (LangSmith MCP for traces, Jira MCP for issue creation) to cluster signals and draft fixes autonomously before human review.
source_title: Observing And Testing CX Agents | Interrupt 26

claim: Every signal matters: thumbs-down feedback, error traces, low-confidence predictions are all actionable signals, not noise—systems that ignore them lose adoption
actionable: Capture and act on all user feedback and model confidence signals; treat feedback as quality data that drives continuous improvement.
source_title: Observing And Testing CX Agents | Interrupt 26

claim: Treat evals as tests in the pipeline (versioned in code, not in dashboards or Slack)—this ensures regressions are caught and component-level + end-to-end analysis both run
actionable: Version eval datasets alongside code; run both component and end-to-end evals in CI/CD to prevent regressions in production agent deployments.
source_title: Observing And Testing CX Agents | Interrupt 26

claim: MCPs are the integration layer between agents and tools—using MCPs as the standard interface allows swapping backends without changing the front-end agent logic
actionable: Use MCPs to abstract agent interactions with external systems (LangSmith, Jira, Git) to enable flexibility in backend changes.
source_title: Observing And Testing CX Agents | Interrupt 26

claim: Build semantic routers that derive context from real-time environment data and historical patterns to route to specialized agents, rather than relying on detailed prompts with insufficient context
actionable: When prompt context is insufficient, build a semantic router that reads environmental context, historical data, and best practices to select the right specialized agent.
source_title: Observing And Testing CX Agents | Interrupt 26

claim: Use guardrails in semantic routing to filter harmful user inputs before routing—this prevents contamination of agent selection logic and improves consistency
actionable: Run guardrails before semantic routing to filter toxic input, ensuring the router makes principled decisions on clean signals.
source_title: Observing And Testing CX Agents | Interrupt 26

claim: At 16M interactions/year scale with 153K concurrent requests, observability infrastructure (like LangSmith) becomes critical—treat it as infrastructure and observe the observer
actionable: Monitor observability systems themselves (e.g., via Splunk) to catch infrastructure failures before they cascade.
source_title: Observing And Testing CX Agents | Interrupt 26
